data.title: Chip stocks mixed after Analog Devices' Q2 results
data.description:
Chip stocks were mixed on Thursday after Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) reported its second-quarter results.

Shares of Analog Devices fell about 4% despite second-quarter results and third-quarter outlook exceeding analysts' expectations.

Meanwhile, related stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) rose about 3%, while Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) was largely flat but among the gainers. Texas Instruments (TXN) fell nearly 2% and Microchip Technology (MCHP) dipped around 1%. Intel (INTC) was largely flat but in the red on Thursday.

AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) rose about 1%. Broadcom (AVGO) was also up around 1%. Qualcomm (QCOM) dipped about 2%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) was also in the red.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) climbed about 1%, while GlobalFoundries (GFS) was largely flat but in the red.

Chip equipment makers: KLA (KLAC) slipped nearly 1%, while Applied Materials (AMAT) was largely flat but in the red. Dutch company ASML (ASML) and Lam Research (LRCX) were also largely flat but among the gainers.

data.title: Lightspeed Commerce stock slides after Q4 earnings fall short, mixed guidance
data.description:
Lightspeed Commerce (NYSE:LSPD) shares gapped down 7% in Thursday late morning trading after delivering worse-than-expected fiscal Q4 2025 earnings as well as mixed guidance for the year.

The fintech company sees FY2026 revenue growth of 10%-12%, (vs. 10.3% average analyst estimate), annual gross profit growth of ~14% (vs. 11.1% Visible Alpha estimate) and annual adjusted EBITDA of $68M-$72M (vs. $72.8M Visible Alpha consensus).

For fiscal Q1 2026, it expects revenue of $185M-$290M, in line with the $284.5M Wall Street consensus; Y/Y gross profit growth of about 13%, exceeding the 10.2% Visible Alpha estimate; and adjusted EBITDA of $14M-$16M, missing the $16.4M Visible Alpha estimate.

"Fiscal 2026 will be dedicated to growing locations, expanding software revenue and enhancing Adjusted EBITDA profitability," said Founder and CEO Dax Dasilva.

Turning to fiscal Q4 2025 results, adjusted EPS of $0.10 slid from $0.12 in the prior quarter and rose from $0.06 a year before, compared with the $0.11 consensus. Including certain items, such as goodwill impairment and share-based compensation, the company recorded a net loss of $0.21 per share.

Total revenue for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, was $253.4M, down from $280.1M in Q3 and advanced from $230.2M in last year's Q4. The latest print surpassed the $251.3M expected.

Total operating expenses surged to $691.9M in Q4 from $149.9M in Q3 and $141.0M a year earlier, mainly reflecting a $556.4M goodwill impairment.

Adjusted EBITDA was $12.9M vs. $16.6M in Q3 and $4.39M in the year-ago period.

Gross profit of $111.8M compared with $115.9M in the previous quarter and gained 12% Y/Y.

In the last 12 months, Lightspeed (NYSE:LSPD) has bought back about 18.7M shares, or ~12% of total shares outstanding, for proceeds of ~$219M.

data.title: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac shares surge after Trump suggests privatization
data.description:
Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) stock jumped 23% and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) surged 24% in morning trading after President Donald Trump overnight indicated that the time would  "seem right" to release the two mortgage giants from government conservatorship.

Late Tuesday, Trump said he's giving "very serious consideration" to releasing the government-sponsored enterprises, which guarantee a majority of the country's home mortgages. He said he'll make a decision in the near future.

While Mark Calabria, who led the Federal Housing Finance Agency during Trump's first term, came up with a plan to release the GSEs, the two companies hadn't built up enough capital and there wasn't a decision on what would happen with the government's holdings of Fannie (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie's (OTCQB:FMCC) preferred shares.

Earlier this week, shares in the two companies climbed after current FHFA director William J. Pulte said at a mortgage industry conference that he found "a couple hundred million of dollars" of savings

He also said called for the privatization of Fannie (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie (OTCQB:FMCC) at the same conference. In the past month, Fannie Mae stock jumped 45%, while Freddie Mac's soared 35%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wall Street searches for direction as U.S. bond sell-off eases up
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages searched for direction on Thursday, while a bruising bond sell-off from the previous session eased up. The focus remained on President Trump's "One Big Beautiful" tax bill, which was narrowly passed by the U.S. House.

Stock index futures showed little reaction to the favorable vote, and the major averages eventually opened mixed. Moves have remained small since. Approaching midday, the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) was last +0.08% at 5,849.42 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.12% at 41,909.15 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.50% at 18,967.71 points.

U.S. Treasury yields had stabilized on Thursday after soaring on Wednesday, as traders had dumped bonds amid concerns over the new tax bill's effects on the ballooning U.S. fiscal deficit. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 4 basis points to 4.56%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.00%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.  

Following days of marathon meetings and intense negotiations, including Republican efforts that lasted through the night, lawmakers early this morning voted 215-214 in favor of the bill that contains more than 1,000 pages and proposes about $3.8T in tax cuts, including the extension of those enacted by U.S. President Trump's Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) in 2017. 

But worries over the implications of the bill on the U.S. fiscal deficit have been on top of investors' minds.

"The die was cast here the moment both presidential tickets agreed to extend TCJA. That keeps revenues at 17% GDP as spending continues inexorable rise & deficits in 6-7% range. The issue is that GOP has taken off the table vast majority of spending progs. (Democrats) have no plan either," Donald Schneider, deputy head of U.S. policy at Piper Sandler, said on X (formerly Twitter) on Wednesday.

"GOP is doing something quite difficult now which is enacting real entitlement cuts and real tax increases. So they are demonstrating credibility that it can be done. But no one should be surprised that deficits will linger in 6%+ territory," Schneider said. 

"Fiscal outlook is basically set at this point. Resolution of the crisis will be left to next President in 2029. Unless both parties do total 180 on entitlement reform," he added. 

Though the tax bill dominated headlines on Thursday, there was also some economic data to focus on. The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week slipped. Meanwhile, S&P Global's flash survey for May showed U.S. business activity growth and expectations for future output improving from April lows. Additionally, U.S. existing home sales unexpectedly dipped in April. 

Turning to active movers, solar stocks were crushed as the House passage of the tax bill hastens a potential end to key clean energy credits. Enphase Energy (ENPH) slumped -19.1% and was the top percentage loser on the S&P 500 (SP500).

data.title: Japan could trigger a ‘global financial market Armageddon’ – SG
data.description:
Global financial markets could face a dramatic shift triggered by Japan’s bond market turmoil, according to Société Générale.

Albert Edwards, chief global strategist at Société Générale, warned that recent developments in Japan may signal the end of the favorable investment environment established since the 2008 Global Financial Crisis, potentially reshaping global financial landscapes in the coming months.

Japanese long-term government bond yields are experiencing an explosive rise, with a recent 20-year JGB auction described as “the worst since 1987,” according to market observers.

“The cracks in global bond markets just turned into fractures,” wrote EndGame Macro on X, highlighting how bids collapsed while yields spiked to record highs for longer maturities, suggesting this represents not merely a technical failure but potentially the “first sovereign domino tipping over.”

Japan’s bond market has functioned as the “keystone” of global yield suppression, with Japanese institutions historically supporting markets through yen-funded carry trades and substantial foreign bond purchases, particularly U.S. Treasuries, Edwards said.

This era appears to be ending as capital returns to Japan while the Bank of Japan loses control of its long-end curve and FX-hedged returns on Treasuries become deeply negative, according to the analysis.

The unwinding of these massive carry trades could create a “loud sucking sound” in U.S. financial assets if higher JGB yields continue to attract Japanese investors back home, the strategist warns. Both U.S. treasury (GOVI), (US10Y), (US2Y), (US30Y) and equity markets (SP500), (DJI), (COMP:IND) remain vulnerable after being inflated by Japanese capital flows, with America’s fiscal situation adding another layer of concern, described by Edwards as a “ticking fiscal time-bomb.”

Economic data reinforces the pressure on Japanese yields, with Japanese core CPI inflation now exceeding that of the U.S. and eurozone. This inflation situation increases pressure on the Bank of Japan to potentially raise rates and reduce its balance sheet, with even moderate quantitative easing tapering already creating significant demand gaps in the JGB market, according to Bloomberg in an article.

data.title: Southwest Airlines issues warning on Q2 revenue forecast
data.description:
• Shares of Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) surrendered early gains and traded at a loss Thursday after the company warned that demand across the industry has not reached “an inflection point.”
  • For Q2, the company expects to see unit revenue ~6 points worse than what was expected at the beginning of the year.
  • Regarding EBIT contribution, Southwest (NYSE:LUV) sees $1.8B this year and over $4B next year. This closely aligns to the company's forecast in Q1 results last month. 
  • The projections drove the stock briefly below its 200-day moving average and into the red for a fourth straight day.
  • The remarks were made at an industry event hosted by Wolfe Research.
  • Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) reports Q2 results after the market close on July 24.

data.title: Instil Bio shares rise on immunotherapy trial updates
data.description:
Instil Bio (NASDAQ:TIL) shares moved higher after the company, alongside its partner ImmuneOnco Biopharmaceuticals, announced key updates to the clinical development strategy for their experimental bispecific antibody ‘2510.

Instil plans to launch a Phase 1b/2 trial of ‘2510 in the U.S. by the end of 2025, pending regulatory approval. The study will focus on dose optimization in relapsed or refractory solid tumors as a monotherapy. This new trial replaces an earlier plan to test ‘2510 in combination with chemotherapy for first-line non-small cell lung cancer.

Meanwhile in China, ImmuneOnco expects to complete enrollment of about 60 patients in a Phase 2 trial testing ‘2510 with chemotherapy in first-line NSCLC by Q3 2025, with initial safety and efficacy results anticipated in the second half of that year. A Phase 3 trial in the same setting is targeted to begin in mid-2026.

Early data from ‘2510 monotherapy in relapsed/refractory NSCLC in China showed a 23% objective response rate, comparable to other bispecific antibodies in similar stages of development.

The stock is up 57% so far today.

data.title: Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Index contracts slightly in May
data.description:
The Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Composite Index improved slightly to -3, but still represents a slight contraction, in May from -4 in April, the district Federal Reserve Bank said on Thursday. 

"Nondurable manufacturing declined—driven by food manufacturing — while durable manufacturing was mostly flat, with increases in metals and furniture manufacturing and decreases in electronic and transportation equipment," the Kansas City Fed said.

The index also reflected moderate declines in production, volume of shipments, and new orders this month. Backlogs of orders fell substantially, and employment increased modestly. Inventories were mostly flat.

One respondent commented, "Year started strong but seems to be tapering off for at least the near term." Another  said. "Trade policy continues to impact business. The constant uncertainty around overall policy is negative for consumers and for us."

More on the US Economy

  • Fundamental Reasons For A Bullish View Of The Economy Beyond 2025
  • Chicago Fed National Activity Index reverses to negative territory in April
  • Existing home sales unexpectedly edge down in April
  • Trump's 'big beautiful' budget bill passes House vote
  • Tax cuts, trade clarity will boost U.S. economy in H2 2025, White House economist Hassett says

data.title: BJ's Wholesale Club maintains 2% to 3.5% comp sales growth guidance for 2025 as membership hits record high
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. (BJ) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Bob Eddy, Chairman and CEO, opened the call by stating, “We grew net sales by nearly 5%, and we managed the business well, resulting in operating income and net income growth of 27% and 35%, respectively.” He highlighted ongoing momentum in traffic and market share, especially in clubs and gas stations, and announced that comparable club sales excluding gas grew by 3.9%, led by a 13th consecutive quarter of traffic growth. Membership continues to grow, with higher tier membership penetration surpassing 40% for the first time.
  • Eddy reported “perishables, grocery, and sundries division delivered more than 4% comp growth in the first quarter, with unit volumes increasing across all three divisions,” and noted strong growth in private label brands. He introduced the expansion of the Fresh 2.0 initiative from produce to meat and seafood, after successful pilots and market studies, to further drive trip frequency and basket size.
  • Significant progress was reported in digital convenience, with digitally enabled comp sales growing 35% year-over-year in the first quarter. Technology investments have improved order fulfillment efficiency, reducing pick time by over 45%.
  • Eddy announced the opening of five new clubs in the quarter, including the first Staten Island location, and reaffirmed plans to open 25 to 30 new clubs over the next two years.
  • Laura Felice, CFO, stated, “Net sales in the first quarter were $5 billion, increasing 4.7% year-over-year. Merchandise comp sales, which exclude gas sales increased by 3.9% year-over-year, led by traffic and units.” She further noted that “membership fee income, or MFI, grew 8.1% to approximately $120.4 million in the first quarter, led by strong membership acquisition and retention across the chain.”

Outlook

  • Felice reiterated unchanged full-year guidance: “2% to 3.5% comp sales growth, excluding gas and $4.10 to $4.30 in adjusted earnings per share.” She acknowledged a wider range of potential outcomes due to cost and consumer spending uncertainty and expects first quarter comps ex-gas to be the high watermark for the year.
  • Management indicated the company will continue to exercise cost discipline while investing in long-term value and growth for members.

Financial Results

  • The company reported first quarter net sales of $5 billion, a 4.7% increase year-over-year. Merchandise gross margins increased by approximately 30 basis points year-over-year, with minimal tariff-related impact in the quarter.
  • SG&A expenses were approximately $760.9 million, resulting in about 10 basis points of deleverage as a percentage of net sales, mainly from investments in new club openings and growth initiatives.
  • First quarter earnings per share were $1.13 and adjusted earnings per share was $1.14. Inventory levels declined 2% on a per club basis. Capital expenditures in the first quarter were approximately $140.5 million.

Q&A

  • Peter Benedict, Baird, asked about the real estate strategy and target markets for club expansion. Eddy responded that success in new clubs has driven confidence to accelerate growth, and Bill Werner (EVP) added, “the pipeline is as big as it’s ever been,” with aggressive expansion in both new and existing markets.
  • Kate McShane, Goldman Sachs, inquired about changes to the long-term algorithm amid membership growth. Eddy said, “we don’t necessarily see that changing all that much at this point in time, particularly given the level of uncertainty out there in the market.”
  • Robert Ohmes, Bank of America, probed margin investments and comp cadence. Felice emphasized that value remains central, and the first half of the year is expected to be stronger than the back half, consistent with prior guidance.
  • Michael Baker, D.A. Davidson, requested details on Fresh 2.0’s impact on meat and seafood. Eddy explained produce and meat are of comparable size, with early positive results in meat, though full impact is yet to be seen.
  • Edward Kelly, Wells Fargo, asked about tariff risks in guidance. Eddy confirmed, “today’s fair situation and maybe whatever could happen tomorrow, all of that is sort of embedded in our guidance ranges.”
  • Several analysts raised questions on digital convenience, general merchandise outlook, relocations, and the impact of construction costs on new club growth, with management emphasizing ongoing investments and operational efficiencies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on margin investments, expansion strategy, and tariff exposure. Inquiries centered on potential risks but acknowledged the company’s strong momentum.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone, consistently referencing the ability to adapt to a dynamic environment. Bob Eddy’s statements, such as, “I’m confident in our ability to execute through the near term together and more convinced than ever that we’re set up for long-term sustainable growth,” reinforced this confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a similar tone, with management slightly more cautious amid increased uncertainty but still projecting confidence in the strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for comp sales growth (2% to 3.5%) and adjusted earnings per share ($4.10 to $4.30) remained unchanged from the previous quarter, though management highlighted a wider range of potential outcomes.
  • Membership penetration in higher tiers surpassed 40%, up over 100 basis points sequentially, compared to nearly 40% in the previous quarter.
  • Digitally enabled comp sales growth accelerated to 35% year-over-year, versus 26% in the prior quarter.
  • Fresh 2.0 expanded from produce to meat and seafood, whereas the previous quarter focused primarily on produce.
  • The company opened five new clubs in the first quarter, with a stated plan to open 25 to 30 clubs over the next two years, consistent with earlier guidance.
  • Management’s tone remained confident but acknowledged greater market uncertainty, particularly regarding tariffs and cost pressures.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited broader economic uncertainty and potential upward cost pressures, including tariffs, as ongoing risks.
  • Eddy highlighted that BJ’s is “less impacted by imports than many of our competitors,” but noted the situation is dynamic and may affect pricing.
  • Felice warned that “the range of potential outcomes have become wider since we issued our annual guidance,” citing unpredictable costs and consumer behavior.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of tariffs, margin investments, and consumer sentiment on future performance.

Final Takeaway

BJ’s Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. delivered a strong first quarter, marked by record membership metrics, robust comparable sales growth, and rising penetration of premium membership tiers. The company reaffirmed its outlook for 2025, maintaining guidance for comp sales growth and adjusted EPS despite acknowledging a more dynamic and uncertain environment. Strategic investments in Fresh 2.0, digital convenience, and accelerated club openings are expected to drive long-term growth, while disciplined cost management and adaptability remain at the forefront as BJ’s navigates evolving economic conditions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Urban Outfitters rallies on Q1 results, upgrade; sees need to 'gently' raise prices
data.description:
For the first time in three years, Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN) reported comparable sales growth in all three banners, including an inflection to positive growth in the Urban Outfitters brand. This contributed to consolidated same store sales growth of 5% for the first quarter, beating expectations of 3.4% growth, and contributing to a 21% rally in shares during Thursday’s trading session.

“We see a 5-pronged top/bottom-line growth flywheel supported by ‘core stability’ at Anthropologie/Free People brands, and opportunity to grow 3x the Free People Movement revenue base versus today,” J.P. Morgan’s Matthew Boss writes in Thursday’s research note, driving an upgrade to Overweight from Neutral with a corresponding bump of 24% to URBN’s price target.

Tariffs are expected to only have a “minimal” negative impact for the second quarter and a 20 basis points headwind to gross margins in the second half. This can be attributed to the company’s efforts over the last several years to diversify its supply chain and “dual source” most of its own brands. This enables Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN) to shift production out of one country and into another if necessary, which means no single country accounts for more than 25% of production, with China representing less than 5%.

Still, with production concentrated in India, Vietnam, and Turkey, the tariff risk could change suddenly. Accordingly, CEO Richard Hayne said the company would “gently and sparingly raise some prices,” on the company’s earnings call.

“Please note that any price increases will be very strategic, protecting opening price points and only targeting areas where we believe we could raise prices without affecting the overall customer experience,” he said, adding that while tariffs will have a minimal negative impact on gross margins, they present a temporary headwind to the business.

“We are confident in our ability to manage through this environment and still achieve 50 to 100 basis points of gross margin improvement for fiscal year 2026.”

This estimate embeds the current tariff rate of 30A% on China and 10% of reciprocal tariffs.

The results launched Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN) shares to a record high.
